sql_findings.query_summary: Identified users with high velocity and fingerprint rotation.
sql_findings.result_summary: Sophie Laurent made 9 withdrawals in 6 days using 9 unique device fingerprints. Carlos Mendez used 3 different fingerprints from a single IP address.

sql_findings.query_summary: Checked for shared device fingerprints across accounts.
sql_findings.result_summary: Fingerprint 'a1b2c3d4e5f6a1b2c3d4e5f6a1b2c3d4' was found linked to both Sophie Laurent and Victor Petrov, confirming account linkage.

sql_findings.query_summary: Analyzed payment method health.
sql_findings.result_summary: Sophie Laurent has 4 cards registered, 3 of which are blacklisted and 3 are unverified, alongside multiple 'card_restricted' transaction failures.

web_references.title: Velocity Threat Signals - Real-time Fraud Pattern Detection
web_references.relevance: Explains how velocity signals and device fingerprints are used to detect rapid carding and brute-force attacks.

web_references.title: What Are Device Fingerprints and How Can They Be Used for Fraud Detection?
web_references.relevance: Describes how device fingerprinting identifies anomalies indicating fraud rings and account takeovers.

sql_findings.query_summary: Detection of 'impossible travel' via IP address changes within a 24-hour window.
sql_findings.result_summary: Customer 74cd8909-2030-55c0-8ec1-9af299a3910a requested withdrawals from 5 different IPs (including 49.36.120.80 and 196.196.45.241) within 12 hours on Feb 8, 2026.

sql_findings.query_summary: Analysis of high-velocity withdrawal attempts from new devices.
sql_findings.result_summary: Customer 851b3a09-1d29-5d95-9257-37fbf678efd2 attempted 5 withdrawals of $400 each in 40 minutes from IP 185.199.108.55, triggering high device and geographic risk scores.

web_references.title: Impossible Travel: How Hunters Detects Account Takeover
web_references.relevance: Explains the 'impossible travel' detector and its role in stopping ATO.

web_references.title: Impossible Travel Detection With IP Data | IPinfo.io
web_references.relevance: Describes how financial services use geolocation data to trigger fraud prevention.

sql_findings.query_summary: Identify customers with high withdrawal-to-deposit ratios and low trade counts.
sql_findings.result_summary: Found multiple customers (e.g., Sophie Laurent, Ahmed Hassan) with ratios near 1.0 and <= 2 trades. Many withdrawals were for the near-exact amount of the initial deposit.

sql_findings.query_summary: Check for shared payment methods across the suspicious accounts.
sql_findings.result_summary: Confirmed four distinct pairs of accounts sharing payment credentials (Visa cards and BTC addresses), proving collusion.

sql_findings.query_summary: Investigate shared device fingerprints and IP addresses.
sql_findings.result_summary: Identified a suspicious placeholder fingerprint ('deadbeef...') and a shared legitimate fingerprint used by multiple customers, along with IP overlaps.

web_references.title: Anti-Money Laundering Compliance Guidance - CIRO
web_references.relevance: Describes the 'deposit followed by immediate withdrawal' pattern as a high-risk AML indicator.

web_references.title: Mastering AML Typologies Through Capital Markets - NICE Actimize
web_references.relevance: Identifies 'U-turn' transactions and pass-through behavior in capital markets.

sql_findings.query_summary: Shared device fingerprint analysis across customers.
sql_findings.result_summary: Identified two fingerprints shared by multiple customers, including one shared between Russia and France, and another shared by two Egyptian accounts.

sql_findings.query_summary: Transaction and trading behavior analysis for linked accounts.
sql_findings.result_summary: Confirmed 'tokenistic' trading (trades <$20 lasting seconds) and card testing (multiple 'card_restricted' errors) followed by high-velocity withdrawals (up to $29k in 6 days).

sql_findings.query_summary: IP and geographic consistency check.
sql_findings.result_summary: Found direct IP sharing for the Egyptian cluster and geographic discrepancies (FRA/RUS) for the international cluster.

web_references.title: Investigating Crypto Scams | TRM Labs
web_references.relevance: Describes money mule patterns and rapid fund movement.

web_references.title: 2024 Payment Threats and Fraud Trends Report
web_references.relevance: Discusses payment threats and the use of accomplices on different continents to move funds.

sql_findings.query_summary: Identified customers with high withdrawal-to-deposit ratios and low trade counts.
sql_findings.result_summary: Found 3 primary targets (Hassan, Petrov, Nour) with >95% withdrawal ratios and 0-1 trades.

sql_findings.query_summary: Checked for shared infrastructure (IP, Device, Card) among suspicious accounts.
sql_findings.result_summary: Confirmed Hassan and Nour share IP/Device/Card. Petrov shares Device/Card with a fourth user, Sophie Laurent.

web_references.title: Typologies of money laundering by COAF
web_references.relevance: Describes 'pass-through' accounts and rapid withdrawal behavior as a red flag for layering.

web_references.title: Layering: The Second Stage of Money Laundering
web_references.relevance: Defines the 'Layering' phase of money laundering which involves rapid transfers to obscure the audit trail.

title: Pattern: cross_account
formal_pattern_name: Money Mule Ring / Collusive Fraud Ring

sql_findings.query_summary: Identification of shared recipient accounts across multiple customers.
sql_findings.result_summary: Found recipient account 'EG800002000156789012345678901' shared by two customers (Ahmed Hassan and Fatima Nour).

sql_findings.query_summary: Identification of shared device fingerprints across multiple customers.
sql_findings.result_summary: Found two shared device fingerprints: 'deadbeef...' shared by the Egyptian cluster and 'a1b2...' shared by a second cluster (Sophie Laurent and Victor Petrov).

sql_findings.query_summary: Identification of shared IP addresses across multiple customers.
sql_findings.result_summary: Found IP address '41.44.55.66' shared by the primary Egyptian cluster.

web_references.title: Fraud Ring: Definition, 6 Types, & How to Stop Criminals - Unit21
web_references.relevance: Provides definitions of fraud rings and describes how shared information (IP, device, recipient) links accounts.

web_references.title: The Modern Fraud Ring — And Why Collaborative SARs ... - FiVerity
web_references.relevance: Explains the collaborative nature of modern fraud rings and the importance of identifying shared mule activity.

sql_findings.query_summary: Identify clusters of customers sharing device fingerprints and recipient accounts.
sql_findings.result_summary: Found a cluster of 2 customers sharing device 'deadbeef...' and recipient account 'EG80...'.

sql_findings.query_summary: Analyze registration and withdrawal timing for the identified cluster.
sql_findings.result_summary: Confirmed that both customers registered and withdrew at the exact same second, sharing the same IP address.

web_references.title: Application Value Of Device Fingerprinting In Payment Fraud Prevention
web_references.relevance: Explains how device fingerprints are used to link seemingly unrelated accounts into a fraud ring.

web_references.title: Collusion & Money Mule Networks: The Backbone of Modern Fraud
web_references.relevance: Describes the characteristics of money mule networks and coordinated withdrawal patterns.

sql_findings.query_summary: Identified new accounts with high withdrawal-to-deposit ratios and minimal trading.
sql_findings.result_summary: Found 4 accounts registered within 30 days with >$10k in withdrawals and <5 trades. One account had 54 card errors.

sql_findings.query_summary: Checked for shared device fingerprints and IP addresses.
sql_findings.result_summary: Confirmed that multiple flagged accounts share the same device fingerprints and IP addresses, and are hitting 'card_restricted' error codes.

sql_findings.query_summary: Analyzed withdrawal recipients for shared endpoints.
sql_findings.result_summary: Discovered that two separate accounts are withdrawing to the same recipient name ('Mohamed Nour'), confirming a fraud ring.

web_references.title: Credit Card and Debit Card Fraud - OCC.gov
web_references.relevance: Describes the 'Carding' process where stolen cards are tested on platforms.

web_references.title: 'Card Cracking' Scams | American Bankers Association
web_references.relevance: Explains 'Card Cracking' where accounts are used as conduits for fraudulent funds.

sql_findings.query_summary: Identify customers sharing device fingerprints and check for IP/Registration country mismatches.
sql_findings.result_summary: Found two fingerprints shared by multiple users. One shared by a Russian and a French account; another shared by two Egyptian accounts.

sql_findings.query_summary: Analyze withdrawal behavior (timing and recipients) for shared-device accounts.
sql_findings.result_summary: Detected simultaneous withdrawal requests (to the second) for the FRA/RUS cluster and a shared recipient name/account for the EGY cluster.

web_references.title: Block multi-accounting with Device Intelligence - SEON Docs
web_references.relevance: Explains how shared device fingerprints and IP mismatches are primary indicators of multi-accounting and account farming.

web_references.title: AI-Driven Fraud Detection Systems in Fintech - IJRPR
web_references.relevance: Discusses how coordinated fraud rings use shared devices and behavioral triangulation to commit identity fraud.
